skill.name: firecrawl-map
skill.description: Discover and list all URLs on a website, with optional search filtering. Use this skill when the user wants to find a specific page on a large site, list all URLs, see the site structure, find where something is on a domain, or says "map the site", "find the URL for", "what pages are on", or "list all pages". Essential when the user knows which site but not which exact page.
skill.summary: Discover and list all URLs on a website with optional search filtering to find specific pages.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 19 potential issues, but all are false positives from documentation text analysis. The external_commands patterns are CLI usage examples in markdown, not code execution. The network reference is an example URL in documentation. The filesystem references are markdown relative links to other skill documentation files. No actual security risks detected.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains CLI Examples
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash command examples using firecrawl CLI. These are documentation examples, not executable code in the skill itself.

content.user_title: Map and discover all URLs on any website
content.value_statement: Finding specific pages on large websites is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automatically discovers and lists all URLs on a domain with intelligent search filtering to locate exactly what you need.
actual_capabilities: Discover all URLs on a website domain
Filter URLs by search query to find specific pages
Export URL lists in JSON or text format
Limit output to specific number of URLs
limitations: Requires firecrawl CLI to be installed and configured
Cannot access password-protected or authenticated pages
Results depend on the target site being accessible online
Large sites may require multiple requests to map completely

use_cases.title: Documentation Site Navigation
use_cases.description: Quickly find the exact documentation page you need on large technical documentation sites by searching for relevant keywords.

use_cases.title: Competitive Website Analysis
use_cases.description: Map competitor websites to understand their site structure, content organization, and available product pages.

use_cases.title: Pre-Scraping Site Discovery
use_cases.description: Generate a complete URL inventory before running bulk scraping operations to ensure comprehensive coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Basic URL Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Map all URLs on example.com and show me the first 50 results
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of a website's structure

prompt_templates.title: Search-Filtered Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find all pages on docs.example.com that mention authentication or login
prompt_templates.scenario: Locating specific documentation within a large site

prompt_templates.title: Export for Further Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Map example.com with subdomains included and export as JSON to .firecrawl/sitemap.json
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a site inventory for automated processing

prompt_templates.title: Targeted Page Location
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the firecrawl.dev site for pricing pages and return the exact URL
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding a specific page type when you do not know the path

output_examples.input: Map https://docs.example.com with search term "API reference"
Map https://example.com with limit 10 and JSON output

best_practices: Use the --search parameter to narrow results on large sites
Start with a small limit to test connectivity before mapping entire sites
Combine map with scrape skill to retrieve content from discovered URLs
Use JSON output format when processing URLs programmatically
anti_patterns: Do not map extremely large sites without setting a reasonable limit
Avoid running map repeatedly in quick succession to prevent rate limiting
Do not use map as a substitute for scraping when you need page content
Avoid mapping sites that explicitly prohibit crawling in robots.txt

faq.question: What is the difference between map and crawl?
faq.answer: Map discovers and lists URLs on a site without fetching content. Crawl retrieves and processes the actual page content. Use map first to find URLs, then scrape or crawl to get content.

faq.question: Can this skill access password-protected pages?
faq.answer: No. The map skill only discovers publicly accessible URLs. Authentication-protected pages will not appear in results unless the firecrawl CLI is configured with appropriate credentials.

faq.question: How do I include subdomain URLs in results?
faq.answer: Add the --include-subdomains flag to your map command to discover URLs across all subdomains of the target domain.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: Results can be output as plain text (default) or JSON using the --json flag. You can also save to a file using the --output or -o option.

faq.question: How accurate is the --search filter?
faq.answer: The search filter performs substring matching on discovered URLs. It filters results after discovery, so all URLs are still crawled but only matching ones are returned.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many URLs can be discovered?
faq.answer: You can set a maximum with --limit. Without a limit, the skill will attempt to discover all accessible URLs, which may take longer on large sites.
